groups.title: Parts of the hotel
groups.question_title: What problem does Mona point out about each part of the hotel where she interned?
groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
What problem does Mona point out about each part of the hotel where she interned?
Write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 21-26.

Questions 27-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: How does the experience benefit Mona?

options.title: A
options.content: It is good for her future job.

options.title: B
options.content: It is good for her dissertation.

options.title: C
options.content: It can let her know something about the specific job.

questions.title: Why does the tutor want Mona to do the presentation?

options.title: A
options.content: Because she can do it skilfully.

options.title: B
options.content: Because she wants to do it herself.

options.title: C
options.content: Because she can show other students how to do a presentation.

questions.title: What does Mona suggest Tom do?

options.title: A
options.content: talk to the university's career centre

questions.title: What is Tom's plan for his own essay?

options.title: A
options.content: to study about the rise of five-star hotels

options.title: C
options.content: to do research into how the hotel industry works

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Hello Tom, sorry I'm late.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Hi Mona, no problem.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I heard you've finished the essay for the management course.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Which company did you focus on?

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: It was finished last week.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: I chose the hotel where I interned last summer and mainly concentrated on my work experience there.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Could you give me some details?

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I'm interested.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Well, during my three-month internship, I observed every part of the hotel carefully each day and also talked with some of my colleagues.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: I've found some problems in different parts of the hotel and made a conclusion in the essay.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Let me start from the reception desk.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: I noticed the staff there kept doing the same thing every day, like answering the phone, helping guests with check-in and check-out, etc.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: There was nothing special for them.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: That must be very boring.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Yeah I think so.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: And in the restaurant, the employees told me that they must obey the rules exactly.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: For example, if they don't wear uniforms during working hours, 20% of their daily wages will be deducted.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: And then about the coffee bar.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: During peak hours, I was asked to help in the coffee bar several times, as a shortage of hands always made many customers wait long for ordering, eating or paying the bills.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Their own staff could not control customer flow.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Oh, that seems a little messy.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Yes, definitely.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: And the place where I worked was a shop selling towels, and pyjamas as well as souvenirs.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Every day I had to be there from 9 am to 6 pm.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: My duty was to set up the window display and serve the customers.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: At first it seemed interesting, but later I found this work was a bit tough.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Every night when I got off work, I was nearly exhausted.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: What I wanted was just a good rest and sleep.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Doesn't sound like an easy job.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: It definitely wasn't.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Then there was the personnel office.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: I went there on the first day when I arrived at the hotel.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: As I was not familiar with everything, I asked the staff several questions.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: But the employee of the personnel office seemed a bit annoyed and started complaining, which gave me a very bad impression.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Lastly, as regards cleaning, it wasn't too bad compared with other parts of the hotel, except the vacuum cleaners, which were probably bought such a long time ago that the sound seemed too loud when they were working.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: But fortunately, the hotel hasn't received any complaint from the customers so far.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I think you've benefited a lot from the internship.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Yeah, absolutely.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: In the beginning, I just wanted to complete the essay for the course.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: But in the end, I found it has given me some practical examples which are helpful to what I intend to do after graduation, as you know I've always wanted to do something related to hotel management.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Due to the time limitation, I still haven't got a clear idea of some specific work positions.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Well, I heard you're going to give us a presentation of your essay next time in class?

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Yes, actually I'm not so good at giving a presentation.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: But the tutor says that's just what he wants me to do, to know what is important when doing a presentation, like how to attract the audience, make eye contact and make gestures, etc., and other students can learn from my presentation.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I'm looking forward to it.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: You know, now I'm only in the second year of the course, and I have to write an essay next semester.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: But I have no idea about where I can do an internship.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: There're several hotels which I'm interested in.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Do you think I should call their managers?

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Well, I suggest you'd better not because you're more likely to be rejected by them.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: There is a career office on campus that has got a list of companies offering internship opportunities to students.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: The office will help you contact where you want to go.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: You just have to prepare your personal information like the CV.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Oh, don't forget to provide a reference from the tutor.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Great!

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I've got it.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: So, have you got any plans for your essay?

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I've noticed that the hotel industry is developing at a high speed.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: People are more and more concerned about the quality of accommodation.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: What I'm struck by is the numerous statistics showing the ever-increasing number of five-star hotels, so I intend to carry out extensive research on this aspect.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: I'm thinking about using video as a research method, but I haven't decided yet.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: I think it's an interesting topic.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: Anyway, if you have any questions later, I will be happy to help.

transcript.speaker: Tom
transcript.en: Thanks a lot, Mona.

transcript.speaker: Mona
transcript.en: You're welcome.

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 reception。
题目解析：配对题的核心考点在于对同义替换的识别，包括题干和选项在录音中发生的同义替换。本部分 6 道题的题干均为酒店的一部分，其名称不易发生替换，精确定位后重点听选项的同义替换即可。本题定位词 reception 原词重现后，紧跟答案信息：“I noticed the staff there kept doing the same thing every day, like answering the phone, helping guests with check-in and check-out, etc. There was nothing special for them.”其中 doing the same thing every day 以及 nothing special 为选项 C 中 daily routine 的具体展开替换，routine 意为“常规，惯例”。因此答案为 C。

22.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 restaurant。
题目解析：本题难度不大，定位词 restaurant 出现后，答案信息随即出现：“... the employees told me that they must obey the rules exactly.”其中 must obey the rules exactly 替换了选项 B 中的 very strict。之后 Mona 还通过举例进行了具体说明。因此答案为 B。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 coffee bar。
题目解析：听到定位词 coffee bar 之后可锁定答案句：“... as a shortage of hands always made many customers wait long for ordering, eating or paying the bills.”只需听出 a shortage of hands 与选项 D 中的 lacks sufficient staff 为同义替换即可。还可通过之后听到的“Their own staff could not control customer flow.”来判断，这也是在表达人手不够。因此答案为 D。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 shop。
题目解析：本题定位词 shop 距离答案信息较远，中间描述了工作的时间和内容，不过转折信号词 but 的出现预示着答案即将出现：“... but later I found this work was a bit tough. Every night when I got off work, I was nearly exhausted.”其中 tough 和 exhausted 替换了选项 F 中的 very tiring。因此答案为 F。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 personnel office。
题目解析：同上一题，定位词 personnel office 出现后没有马上出现答案句，依然是通过转折信号词 But 来锁定答案句：“But the employee of the personnel office seemed a bit annoyed and started complaining ...”其中 a bit annoyed 和 started complaining 替换了选项 A 中的 lacks patience。因此答案为 A。

26.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 cleaning。
题目解析：本题不难，定位词 cleaning 和答案信息出现在同一句中：“... which were probably bought such a long time ago that the sound seemed too loud when they were working.”其中 the sound seemed too loud 替换了选项 E 中的 noisy。因此答案为 E。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 benefit；提示词 Mona。
题目解析：本题可通过 Tom 的话来定位，提示词暗示答案会出现在 Mona 的回答中。首先出现了选项 B 中 dissertation 的对应信息 essay，不过随后出现了 But，可排除选项 B，重点听 But 之后的内容：“But in the end, I found it has given me some practical examples which are helpful to what I intend to do after graduation ...”其中 helpful 替换了选项 A 中的 good，what I intend to do after graduation 替换了 her future job。选项 C 中的 know something about the specific job 在录音中被替换为 got a clear idea of some specific work positions，同样作为干扰信息出现，通过其前的否定信号词 haven’t 可排除。因此答案为 A。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 tutor、presentation；提示词 tutor。
题目解析：提示词暗示答案应为 tutor 的意见。定位词 presentation 先出现，接着听到了选项 A 中 skilfully 的否定信息 not so good at，故排除。答案信息依然由 But 引出，同时 tutor 一并出现：“But the tutor says ... and other students can learn from my presentation.”其中 other students can learn from my presentation 对应选项 C 中的 show other students how to do a presentation。选项 B 在录音中未提及。因此答案为 C。

29.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 Mona。
题目解析：本题没有很好的定位词，注意通过对话的推进定位。提示词暗示答案由 Mona 提供。首先听到了选项 C 中的 manager，随即听到 Mona 给出了否定回答，故排除选项 C。接着答案句出现：“There is a career office on campus that has got a list of companies offering internship opportunities to students. The office will help you contact where you want to go.”其中 career office on campus 替换了选项 A 中的 university’s career centre。而选项 B 同样是作为干扰信息，于正确答案后出现，但存在语义偏差，录音中是说需要 tutor 提供一封推荐信，并非选项 B 所说的“去和 tutor 谈论此事”。因此答案为 A。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 plan、essay；提示词 Tom。
题目解析：提示词暗示答案会出现在 Tom 的回答中。定位词 plan 和 essay 同时出现在 Mona 的提问中，注意听 Tom 的回答。首先听到了“... the hotel industry is developing at high speed.”，注意它与选项 C 中的 how the hotel industry works 存在语义偏差。之后听到：“What I’m struck by is ... the ever-increasing number of five-star hotels, so I intend to carry out extensive research on this aspect.”其中 the ever-increasing number of five-star hotels 替换了选项 A 中的 the rise of five-star hotels，intend to 替换了题干中的 plan。最后提到了干扰选项 B，但可通过转折信号词 but 和否定信号词 haven’t decided 排除。因此答案为 A。
